text: We maintain a detailed data inventory listing all elements (user inputs, metadata, logs, identifiers). Each item is mapped to a specific purpose and retention period. We explicitly list data we do NOT collect and justify necessity for each collected element.

Purpose limitation is enforced via data tagging/classification, RBAC/ABAC policies, and policy engines that restrict access by purpose. Organizational controls (training, audits) and contractual terms (DPA) prohibit secondary use without consent.
We conduct quarterly data minimization audits. We regularly remove unnecessary fields and reduce retention where possible. Changes are documented with impact assessments and approvals.

We prevent function creep through change management controls, purpose-based access logs, anomaly detection for unusual data usage, and approval workflows for any new data use. All usage is auditable against declared purposes.
We collect certain user data and logs necessary for service operation, but a complete, auditable inventory with purpose mapping is not consistently maintained.
Data is used only for intended purposes, though enforcement relies primarily on internal policies rather than technical controls.
We review data collection periodically, but there is no formal cadence or documented minimization audit process.
Data may be used to improve services, but controls around secondary use and third-party sharing are not clearly defined.
We collect only what is necessary without specifying data elements or purposes.
We follow standard data collection practices without detailing what is collected or why.
We may use customer data to improve our services without clearly defining scope, consent, or controls.
We are unable to provide a complete list of data elements we collect or how they are used.
